title: Use natural ordering for discovered media playlists when shuffle is off
summary: 0.6.49 fixes discovered file and image playlists so numbered media stays in human sequence when shuffle is off, instead of being forced through plain lexicographic ordering.
changes: Added one shared natural-order media sort helper in `FrameAngelPlayerMediaParity` for sibling and directory playlist discovery.
Direct player media discovery now uses that shared natural sort instead of plain ordinal filename ordering.
Meta harness media discovery uses the same ordering helper so witness and player seams stay aligned.
reasoning: The new image slider behavior was working, but shuffle-off playback was still following a playlist built in plain lexicographic order.
Fixing the discovery sort keeps the transport model unchanged while making numbered media behave like users expect.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Sequentially numbered images now advance in human order when shuffle is off.
The new image slider navigation continues to work on top of the corrected playlist order.
Shuffle behavior remains separate from plain sequential ordering.
knownIssues: This slice does not yet add more aggressive scrub preview updates while dragging.
Slideshow, right-stick volume, and deferred gaze polish remain separate queued slices.
